ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 (OilRig) DNS-tunnel C2 — long-label TXT/A queries (Helminth / DNSpionage / Saitama)
ucs.description: Hunts for the OilRig family's signature DNS-over-resolver C2 channel where the implant chunks command/output into long base32/hex subdomain labels under a single second-level domain. APT34 has reused this primitive across Helminth, DNSpionage, BONDUPDATER, and SAITAMA — chunked encoded data in the leftmost label, sustained query rate, and a small set of target FQDNs from a single host.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Resolution where DNS.query!="" DNS.record_type IN ("A","AAAA","TXT","MX","CNAME") by DNS.src DNS.query DNS.record_type _time host
| `drop_dm_object_name(DNS)`
| rex field=query "^(?<first_label>[^.]+)\.(?<parent>.+)$"
| eval label_len=len(first_label)
| where label_len>=30
| stats count as query_count, dc(first_label) as distinct_subs, dc(parent) as distinct_parents, values(parent) as parent_domains, values(record_type) as record_types, avg(label_len) as avg_label_len, min(_time) as first_seen, max(_time) as last_seen, values(query) as sample_queries by src host
| where distinct_subs>=50 AND avg_label_len>=30 AND distinct_parents<=3
| eval txt_present=if(mvfind(record_types,"TXT")>=0,1,0)
| sort - distinct_subs
ucs.kql:
// APT34 OilRig DNS-tunnel C2 — Helminth/DNSpionage/SAITAMA fingerprint
// Long base32/hex first-label subdomains under a small set of parent domains,
// sustained from one host, often using TXT/A record types.
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(24h)
| where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
| extend QName = tolower(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryName))
| where isnotempty(QName)
| where QName !endswith ".in-addr.arpa" and QName !endswith ".ip6.arpa"
| extend FirstLabel = extract(@"^([^.]+)\.", 1, QName)
| extend ParentDomain = extract(@"([^.]+\.[^.]+)$", 1, QName)
| extend LabelLen = strlen(FirstLabel)
| where LabelLen >= 30
// strip CDN / Microsoft / O365 noise that legitimately uses long labels
| where ParentDomain !in~ ("akamaihd.net","akamaiedge.net","cloudfront.net","azureedge.net","trafficmanager.net","office.com","office365.com","outlook.com","microsoft.com","windows.net","msftncsi.com","live.com","sharepoint.com")
| summarize QueryCount = count(),
            DistinctLabels = dcount(FirstLabel),
            DistinctParents = dcount(ParentDomain),
            ParentDomains = make_set(ParentDomain, 5),
            AvgLabelLen = avg(LabelLen),
            MaxLabelLen = max(LabelLen),
            SampleQueries = make_set(QName, 5),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            Initiators = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 5)
        by DeviceId, DeviceName
| where DistinctLabels >= 50 and AvgLabelLen >= 30 and DistinctParents <= 3
| extend BeaconWindowMin = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where BeaconWindowMin >= 10
| order by DistinctLabels desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT34 OilRig DNS-tunnel C2 — long-label DNS exfil/C2 against a small set of parent domains.
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where isnotempty(Name)
| where Name !endswith ".in-addr.arpa" and Name !endswith ".ip6.arpa"
| extend QName = tolower(Name)
| extend FirstLabel = extract(@"^([^.]+)\.", 1, QName)
| extend ParentDomain = extract(@"([^.]+\.[^.]+)$", 1, QName)
| extend LabelLen = strlen(FirstLabel)
| where LabelLen >= 30
| where ParentDomain !in~ ("akamaihd.net","akamaiedge.net","cloudfront.net","azureedge.net","trafficmanager.net","office.com","office365.com","outlook.com","microsoft.com","windows.net","msftncsi.com","live.com","sharepoint.com")
| summarize QueryCount = count(),
            DistinctLabels = dcount(FirstLabel),
            DistinctParents = dcount(ParentDomain),
            ParentDomains = make_set(ParentDomain, 5),
            AvgLabelLen = avg(LabelLen),
            MaxLabelLen = max(LabelLen),
            SampleQueries = make_set(QName, 5),
            QueryTypes = make_set(QueryType, 5),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
        by Computer, ClientIP
| where DistinctLabels >= 50 and AvgLabelLen >= 30 and DistinctParents <= 3
| extend BeaconWindowMin = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where BeaconWindowMin >= 10
| order by DistinctLabels desc
ucs.rationale: APT34/OilRig is one of the few state actors that has consistently weaponised DNS itself as the primary C2 transport (FireEye M-Trends, Palo Alto Unit 42 reporting on DNSpionage/Helminth, Mandiant SAITAMA report). The long-label + few-parent + sustained-rate fingerprint is shared across their 6+ year toolkit lineage and is much narrower than a generic 'high entropy DNS' template — it specifically t

ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 (OilRig) TwoFace / RGDoor web-shell post-exploitation — w3wp.exe spawning recon binaries
ucs.description: Catches APT34's signature post-exploitation chain on internet-facing IIS / Exchange servers, where their TwoFace and RGDoor web shells use the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) to execute hands-on-keyboard reconnaissance and credential-collection commands. Mandiant and FireEye attribute this exact tradecraft pattern (web-shell → cmd/PowerShell → whoami/net/nltest/systeminfo) to OilRig at multiple Middle-East government and energy victims.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count, values(Processes.process) as cmdlines, values(Processes.user) as users, min(_time) as first_seen, max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","reg.exe","wmic.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe","route.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*whoami*","*net group*","*net user*","*net localgroup*","*systeminfo*","*ipconfig*","*tasklist*","*nltest*","*query user*","*Get-LocalGroup*","*Get-ADUser*","*reg save*")) by host Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.user _time
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval is_recon_cmd=if(match(process,"(?i)(whoami|net\s+(group|user|localgroup)|systeminfo|ipconfig|tasklist|nltest|query\s+user|Get-LocalGroup|Get-ADUser|reg\s+save\s+hklm)"),1,0)
| stats count, dc(process_name) as distinct_children, values(process_name) as children, values(process) as commands, min(first_seen) as first_seen, max(last_seen) as last_seen, sum(is_recon_cmd) as recon_cmd_count by host parent_process_name user
| sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
// APT34 OilRig — TwoFace/RGDoor web shell on IIS/Exchange spawning recon under w3wp.exe
let ReconBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","reg.exe","wmic.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","netstat.exe"]);
let ReconCmdHints = dynamic(["whoami","net group","net user","net localgroup","systeminfo","ipconfig /all","tasklist","nltest /domain_trusts","query user","Get-LocalGroup","Get-ADUser","reg save hklm","vssadmin","net view"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (ReconBins)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any (ReconCmdHints)
| extend AppPool = extract(@"-ap\s+\"?([^\"\s]+)\"?", 1, InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          AppPool,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage = FolderPath,
          ChildBin = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256,
          IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT34 OilRig — TwoFace/RGDoor web shell on IIS/Exchange: w3wp.exe parenting recon binaries.
let ReconImageSuffixes = dynamic(["\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\net.exe","\\net1.exe","\\whoami.exe","\\systeminfo.exe","\\tasklist.exe","\\ipconfig.exe","\\quser.exe","\\nltest.exe","\\reg.exe","\\wmic.exe","\\ping.exe","\\hostname.exe","\\arp.exe","\\route.exe","\\netstat.exe"]);
let ReconCmdHints = dynamic(["whoami","net group","net user","net localgroup","systeminfo","ipconfig /all","tasklist","nltest /domain_trusts","query user","Get-LocalGroup","Get-ADUser","reg save hklm","vssadmin","net view"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| where AccountType == "User" or isempty(AccountType)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName has_any (ReconImageSuffixes))
     or (CommandLine has_any (ReconCmdHints))
| extend AppPool = extract(@"-ap\s+\"?([^\"\s]+)\"?", 1, ParentProcessName)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectUserName,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType, AppPool
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT34 OilRig TwoFace/RGDoor Web Shell - IIS Worker Spawning Recon Binaries
id: a3f4c7d2-8e5b-4f1a-9c2d-7b3e8d1f4a6c
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects w3wp.exe (IIS worker process) spawning command shells or built-in reconnaissance binaries.
  This is the canonical post-exploitation pattern of APT34 (OilRig / Helix Kitten / Hazel Sandstorm)
  TwoFace and RGDoor web shells deployed against internet-facing IIS and Exchange servers.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0049/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/twoface-webshell-persistent-access-point-lateral-movement
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/twoface-webshell-persistent-access-point-for-lateral-movement/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2024/01/15
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1087.002
  - attack.t1069.002
  - attack.g0049
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_child_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\ping.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\arp.exe'
      - '\route.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
  selection_child_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'whoami'
      - 'net group'
      - 'net user'
      - 'net localgroup'
      - 'systeminfo'
      - 'ipconfig /all'
      - 'nltest /domain_trusts'
      - 'query user'
      - 'Get-LocalGroup'
      - 'Get-ADUser'
      - 'reg save hklm'
      - 'vssadmin'
      - 'net view'
  condition: selection_parent and (selection_child_image or selection_child_cmd)
falsepositives:
  - Authenticated SharePoint/Exchange admin scripts run inside an app pool (rare; baseline per host)
  - Application monitoring tools that exec sub-processes from IIS
level: high
ucs.rationale: TwoFace and RGDoor web shells are publicly attributed to APT34 by Mandiant/FireEye and Unit42 — both shells operate in-process under w3wp.exe, so any reconnaissance/credential-collection child process inherits w3wp.exe as its parent. Generic web-shell rules tend to focus on file drops in inetpub paths; this rule keys on the actor-specific behaviour (w3wp.exe → recon binary chain on Exchange/IIS in

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?
